jokes.text: And then the village idiot interrupted the prayer... ruining the feast for one and all.
jokes.setup: Carlton appears to be giving a proper Thanksgiving historical lesson
jokes.punchline: He identifies himself as the village idiot who ruins things
jokes.explanation: Nice self-aware joke that establishes Carlton's earnest but naive character while breaking the fourth wall subtly

jokes.text: Boy, holiday traffic is starting already. People are in such a lousy mood. I learned five new words on the way home. - Did you know what they meant? - I think so. They were accompanied by very helpful hand gestures.
jokes.setup: Will mentions learning new words from holiday traffic
jokes.punchline: The 'helpful hand gestures' implies road rage and profanity
jokes.explanation: Clean way to reference road rage profanity without saying it, solid family-friendly writing

jokes.text: We had to convince her to accept it. She never lets us spend a penny on her. Apparently, that's not a genetic trait.
jokes.setup: Vivian explains Will's mother never lets them spend money on her
jokes.punchline: Philip points out Will doesn't share this trait
jokes.explanation: Sharp character observation with excellent economy, sets up dynamic between Will and his family's values

jokes.text: From the moment they're hatched to the moment they're shrink-wrapped... turkeys are raised to be food, and nothing but food. Hilary, I don't think too many of them had aspirations... to be doctors or lawyers.
jokes.setup: Hilary gives a pretentious speech about turkeys being raised only for food
jokes.punchline: Philip deflates her with the absurd notion of turkey career aspirations
jokes.explanation: Classic Philip deflating Hilary's pretentiousness with logical absurdity, good character interplay

jokes.text: Vi's that way with everybody. Remember what she said about my beard? You misunderstood. She said it made me look like Bigfoot. Which is ridiculous, because nobody knows what Bigfoot really looks like.
jokes.setup: Philip tries to defend Vi's comment about his beard by saying Vivian misunderstood
jokes.punchline: He reveals the actual insult was worse, then uses flawed logic to dismiss it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure and Philip's earnest but flawed reasoning is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Look at my boy. You really have changed. Yeah, I'm handsome. No, you haven't changed.
jokes.setup: Will's mom says he's changed, Will assumes it's a compliment about his looks
jokes.punchline: She immediately shoots down his vanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect mom energy - sets up Will's ego just to knock it down, great economy

jokes.text: He's certainly not taking any food out of your mouth. Starting already. I don't believe it. That's a good one, Mama. I got to use that one. No, you don't make fun of your uncle. That's my job.
jokes.setup: Viola makes a crack about Philip's weight, Will wants to use the joke
jokes.punchline: Viola establishes her exclusive right to roast Philip
jokes.explanation: Establishes family hierarchy and Vi's personality - she can dish it but protects her territory

jokes.text: How's everything at the post office? - They made me a supervisor. - That's wonderful. Honey, they make everybody at the post office a supervisor. You practically have to eat the mail not to get promoted.
jokes.setup: Vi gets congratulated for her promotion to supervisor
jokes.punchline: Philip deflates the achievement by explaining how easy postal promotions are
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about postal service bureaucracy, solid punchline image

jokes.text: And looking less and less like your daddy every day. Ouch. Run for cover, Dad. Where's he going to hide at?
jokes.setup: Vi insults Philip's looks by saying Carlton looks less like him
jokes.punchline: Will's mock concern for Philip, then Vi's follow-up about Philip's size making hiding impossible
jokes.explanation: Double hit on Philip - looks and weight. Will's commentary adds to the pile-on effect

jokes.text: You can take those bags yourself. That man is twice your age. Even though he doesn't look it.
jokes.setup: Vi scolds Will for ordering Geoffrey around due to his age
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about Geoffrey looking young
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Vi's values while still getting in a little dig at Geoffrey

jokes.text: After you, Master William.
jokes.setup: Will was just scolded for ordering Geoffrey around
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey sarcastically emphasizes Will's privilege with formal address
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - passive aggressive but dignified sarcasm in response to being treated as help

jokes.text: He's been wonderful, hasn't he, Philip? Let's just say he takes after you, Vi.
jokes.setup: Vivian tries to give a diplomatic positive answer about Will
jokes.punchline: Philip's 'compliment' is actually saying Will is as difficult as his sister
jokes.explanation: Clever way for Philip to insult Vi while appearing to compliment Will, good diplomatic comedy

jokes.text: Vi, I was dusting the albums this morning and guess what I found. Certainly not dust.
jokes.setup: Vivian sets up finding something while dusting albums
jokes.punchline: Philip points out the obvious contradiction - she wouldn't find dust if she was dusting
jokes.explanation: Weak dad joke territory - obvious wordplay without much payoff

jokes.text: You taught me to dance to this, remember? I didn't teach you to dance like that.
jokes.setup: Vivian credits Vi with teaching her to dance
jokes.punchline: Vi disowns Vivian's current dancing style
jokes.explanation: Classic sibling dynamic - taking credit then immediately distancing when things go wrong

jokes.text: What dance is this? It's called the swim, Ashley. It was very popular in the 1940s. The 1960s, and it's not the swim.
jokes.setup: Carlton confidently explains the dance with wrong decade
jokes.punchline: Will corrects both the decade and the dance name
jokes.explanation: Typical Carlton being confidently wrong about pop culture, Will as the correcting voice of street knowledge

jokes.text: Your daddy knows how to swim. Come on. Swim for her. Come on, Philip. Everybody out of the pool.
jokes.setup: Vi encourages Philip to demonstrate the swim dance
jokes.punchline: Philip's dancing is so bad it clears the 'pool'
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Philip's terrible dancing, the lifeguard whistle punchline works well

jokes.text: The person that invented the elastic waistband... should get the Nobel Prize. - You know what I mean... - Don't start.
jokes.setup: Philip praises elastic waistbands after eating
jokes.punchline: Vivian cuts him off before he can elaborate on his weight issues
jokes.explanation: Relatable fat guy humor, Vivian's interruption shows this is a familiar pattern

jokes.text: Does she still make it? Honey, she passed on. - You're kidding. - Child, she was in her early 100s. You'd have never known by the way she used to swing that baseball bat at me.
jokes.setup: Learning an old lady died at 100+
jokes.punchline: Will recalls her surprising vigor when chasing him with a bat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's troublemaking past, nice contrast between age and energy

jokes.text: And not the worst way to pull my grade up to an 'A.'
jokes.setup: Carlton presents inviting his teacher as pure charity
jokes.punchline: Reveals his ulterior academic motive
jokes.explanation: Predictable reveal of Carlton's true motivation, but fits his calculating character

jokes.text: You shouted, Master William? Yes, Geoffrey. Why don't you have a seat and have some of this delicious peach cobbler... while we clear the table.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey responds to being shouted at with sarcastic formality
jokes.punchline: Will tries to reverse the power dynamic by serving Geoffrey
jokes.explanation: Good power reversal comedy, Will learning lesson about treating help with respect

jokes.text: Your Highness, let us all clear the table. That's a good one. Honey, do I look like I'm telling a joke? Not to me.
jokes.setup: Will sarcastically calls himself 'Your Highness', thinking it's funny
jokes.punchline: Vi's deadly serious response kills the humor, Philip's agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect mom energy - completely deflating Will's attempt at humor with serious tone, Philip wisely staying out

jokes.text: Some guest. Honestly, I've never seen such a lazy bunch in my life... and I work at the post office.
jokes.setup: Vi criticizes the family's laziness
jokes.punchline: Uses post office as the ultimate standard for laziness
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating callback to the post office discussion, great punchline construction

jokes.text: If you want to cut me some flowers, you go get them yourself. Okay, I'll cut you the most beautiful bouquet in the world... but its beauty will pale in comparison to yours. Don't even try that Prince Charming stuff.
jokes.setup: Vi insists Will do his own work, Will responds with over-the-top charm
jokes.punchline: Vi immediately shuts down his smooth talk
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Will's charm doesn't work on his no-nonsense mom, nice callback

jokes.text: 'Cause it's the size of a football field!
jokes.setup: Vi wants Will to mow the lawn like he did back home
jokes.punchline: Will points out the massive size difference between lawns
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective class contrast joke - Philadelphia row house vs Bel-Air mansion grounds

jokes.text: Okay, but I ain't going to cut you no flowers.
jokes.setup: Will gives in to mowing but sets boundaries
jokes.punchline: References the earlier flower-cutting exchange
jokes.explanation: Minor callback that shows Will learning but still trying to maintain some dignity

jokes.text: And they'd rather take orders from a 17-year-old boy?
jokes.setup: Vivian suggests the help might feel uncomfortable taking orders from Vi
jokes.punchline: Vi points out the absurdity - they're taking orders from a teenager instead
jokes.explanation: Sharp logical point that cuts through Vivian's excuse-making, highlights the real class issue

jokes.text: From what I see, all he's learning is how to be a rich kid.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Will's education and advantages
jokes.punchline: Vi's cutting assessment of what Will is actually learning
jokes.explanation: Cuts to the heart of the episode's theme with economy and insight, great character voice

jokes.text: All I know is the kids in this house are getting a free ride... a free ride in a fancy car.
jokes.setup: Vi criticizes the kids getting a free ride
jokes.punchline: Extends the metaphor to their literal fancy transportation
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that reinforces the class theme, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: She said that our kids are spoiled. News flash.
jokes.setup: Vivian is shocked that Vi called their kids spoiled
jokes.punchline: Philip's dry 'news flash' indicates this is obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery format, Philip's matter-of-fact acceptance vs Vivian's denial

jokes.text: Really? Does this sound familiar? 'Daddy, I need $300.' You don't have to get personal.
jokes.setup: Vivian challenges Philip about being too easy on the kids
jokes.punchline: Philip's defensive response when confronted with evidence
jokes.explanation: Good marital comedy - hits too close to home, Philip's weak deflection is relatable

jokes.text: What do you mean, good luck?
jokes.setup: Vivian calls to cancel catering and have the kids cook instead
jokes.punchline: The caterer's ominous 'good luck' response implies disaster
jokes.explanation: Good setup for disaster to come, the unseen caterer's reaction speaks volumes

jokes.text: Geoffrey, it's frozen solid. Really, madam? I thought it was just scared.
jokes.setup: Vivian points out the turkey is frozen solid
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's deadpan suggestion that it might just be frightened
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey moment - perfect deadpan delivery, wordplay on 'frozen' with fear, highly quotable

jokes.text: This is not a drill. It's a fire? My God, I'm going back for my new suede boots. Don't try to stop me. While you're up there, get my lucky drawers!
jokes.setup: Family emergency meeting called, Ashley thinks it's a fire drill
jokes.punchline: Hilary's priorities (suede boots), Will's inappropriate request
jokes.explanation: Good character moments - Hilary's materialism, Will's inappropriate timing, builds chaos

jokes.text: My God, I'm going back for my new suede boots. Don't try to stop me. While you're up there, get my lucky drawers!
jokes.setup: Hilary thinks there's a fire and wants to save her boots
jokes.punchline: Will adds his underwear to the rescue mission
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd priorities in fake emergency with character-appropriate requests

jokes.text: Excuse me? Didn't I just mow Central Park yesterday?
jokes.setup: Vivian says the kids don't do enough work around the house
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggeration of the lawn size and his suffering
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the lawn size complaint, Central Park is a nice comic exaggeration

jokes.text: This is not the time for one of your leftist experiments.
jokes.setup: Carlton objects to having to work for his teacher's visit
jokes.punchline: Frames cooking dinner as political extremism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - turning basic chores into political ideology, excellent character-specific humor

jokes.text: It has a real New Year's resolution feel to it.
jokes.setup: Family discussing new work responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Hilary dismisses it as temporary like failed resolutions
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation that resonates with audience, shows Hilary's cynical intelligence

jokes.text: Since when do I have to ask you twice? Every time, Mommy.
jokes.setup: Vivian uses Vi's tough mom line
jokes.punchline: Ashley's honest answer deflates the authority attempt
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of Vivian's attempt to channel Vi's authority, Ashley's innocence makes it funnier

jokes.text: I mean, she sees me so little... and I'm growing so fast. Don't even try that Prince Charming stuff.
jokes.setup: Will tries to manipulate Vivian with emotional appeal
jokes.punchline: Vivian uses Vi's exact line to shut him down
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Vivian learning from Vi, Will's manipulation doesn't work twice

jokes.text: And so far, you've been getting a free ride, a free ride in a fancy car.
jokes.setup: Vivian lectures Will about his behavior
jokes.punchline: Uses Vi's exact phrase about free rides and fancy cars
jokes.explanation: Direct callback showing Vivian adopting Vi's perspective, perhaps too on-the-nose

jokes.text: Do you realize there's a bump forming back there?
jokes.setup: Hilary is being lectured
jokes.punchline: She's worried about her appearance while wearing a hair bump from the hairnet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary priorities - appearance over everything else, even during crisis

jokes.text: Why don't you just put a yoke around my neck and hitch me to a plow?
jokes.setup: Hilary gets assigned two cooking tasks
jokes.punchline: Dramatically compares light kitchen work to oxen labor
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary melodrama - extreme comparison for mild inconvenience, great imagery

jokes.text: I mean, you guys are getting a fancy ride, a fancy ride in a free car.
jokes.setup: Will tries to lecture his cousins about privilege
jokes.punchline: Gets the callback phrase completely backwards
jokes.explanation: Good comedy of Will trying to sound wise but mangling the catchphrase he's heard repeatedly

jokes.text: We're reading Hamlet. But to make it more relevant to the kids... I'm teaching it to them in a rap version I wrote myself. Oh, Lord.
jokes.setup: Teacher enthusiastically explains his modern Shakespeare approach
jokes.punchline: Vi's immediate dread at what's coming
jokes.explanation: Sets up the cringe comedy to come, Vi's reaction represents the audience's feelings

jokes.text: To be in effect... or not to be in effect? Slamming, man, on the questioning tip.
jokes.setup: Teacher performs his rap version of Hamlet's soliloquy
jokes.punchline: The awkward translation of classic Shakespeare into 90s slang
jokes.explanation: Peak early 90s cringe - well-meaning teacher trying to be cool, genuinely awkward and memorable

jokes.text: It seems that little Mr. Poppin' Fresh doesn't want to come out of his tube. Perhaps this will persuade him.
jokes.setup: Hilary can't get refrigerated dough to open
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizes the dough and threatens violence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hilary's theatrical approach to simple tasks, Pillsbury reference works

jokes.text: All done. Darn it!
jokes.setup: Ashley proudly announces cranberry sauce completion
jokes.punchline: Carlton's immediate frustration implies something went wrong
jokes.explanation: Simple slapstick setup, builds the cooking disaster theme

jokes.text: Carlton, this isn't exactly brain surgery. When something is too dry, what do we do? Cream of mushroom soup.
jokes.setup: Will patronizingly explains basic cooking to Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton's bizarre solution of cream of mushroom soup for everything
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters' inexperience - Will's false confidence, Carlton's random solution

jokes.text: Interesting. I've never had them before with this thick black crust. It's Cajun style.
jokes.setup: Teacher politely comments on the burned yams
jokes.punchline: Hilary tries to pass off burning as intentional cuisine
jokes.explanation: Classic excuse-making for cooking failure, teacher's politeness vs reality

jokes.text: They're just burned on top. Once you get underneath it... they're totally charred.
jokes.setup: Will tries to defend the burned yams
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're even worse than they appear
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - sets up expectation then makes it worse, nice structure

jokes.text: Yeah, you'd never know Ashley dropped it on the floor.
jokes.setup: Teacher compliments Ashley's cranberry sauce
jokes.punchline: Will immediately reveals its floor contamination
jokes.explanation: Classic sibling sabotage - Will can't help but reveal the gross truth

jokes.text: Will is gonna get an 'A' in my English class. Congratulations, baby. Do you have any news for my parents? Not really.
jokes.setup: Teacher announces Will's A grade, Vi congratulates him
jokes.punchline: Carlton desperately hoping for similar news, gets shot down
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Carlton's earlier grade motivation, his desperation vs teacher's bluntness

jokes.text: No! You can have all of that.
jokes.setup: Teacher offers to pass the stuffing
jokes.punchline: Will's panicked prevention of sharing the disaster
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction comedy, builds the disaster dinner atmosphere

jokes.text: It's frozen inside. I'm sorry. I thawed it as much I could. What am I supposed to do, sit on it all day?
jokes.setup: Philip discovers the turkey is still frozen inside
jokes.punchline: Will's defensive suggestion of using body heat
jokes.explanation: Good image of Will sitting on a turkey, shows his inexperience and desperation

jokes.text: I know, 'cause Bigfoot is growling.
jokes.setup: Family discussing getting other food
jokes.punchline: Will references Philip as Bigfoot being hungry
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the earlier Bigfoot reference, Will getting his revenge shot at Philip

jokes.text: That's not true. Mr. Fellows is out there drinking his second cup of stuffing.
jokes.setup: Vivian says the dinner wasn't a total disaster
jokes.punchline: Will points out the stuffing is literally liquid enough to drink
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the earlier stuffing disaster, great image of drinking stuffing

jokes.text: I don't know, I think living in California has made you sensitive.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Vi hurting Vivian's feelings
jokes.punchline: Vi blames California for making Vivian soft
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - East Coast vs West Coast attitude, Vi's no-nonsense perspective

jokes.text: I wouldn't be taking my time in here. Here you go.
jokes.setup: Family still hungry after disaster dinner
jokes.punchline: Vi's matter-of-fact warning about hungry family waiting
jokes.explanation: Good practical Vi moment - knows hungry people are dangerous, especially Philip

jokes.text: They think I'm a genius.
jokes.setup: Will got an A in English class
jokes.punchline: His proud declaration of genius status after the dinner disaster
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between academic success and life skills failure

jokes.text: I wish I could've seen you cooking it. Mom, you definitely would've been crying, straight up.
jokes.setup: Vi wishes she could have seen the cooking disaster
jokes.punchline: Will confirms it would have made her cry
jokes.explanation: Good mother-son bonding over shared humor, Will's honesty about the disaster
